checks_run.summary: 26 tests pass; no failures or errors
All Python files under tests/ compile without error
Both reviewed Python files are properly formatted

repair_verification.justification: Prior F-001 (Medium) is resolved: line 146 now uses assert_file_equals instead of assert_sorted_file_equals; assert_sorted_file_equals has been removed entirely. Prior F-002 (Low) is resolved: assert_diff_prefixes now validates the full line format with specific ERE patterns for added/removed lines (^[\+\-]\ [A-Za-z0-9._/-]+=.*$) and changed lines (^~\ [A-Za-z0-9._/-]+:\ .*\ ->\  .*$), not merely the leading prefix character. No new Critical or High findings were introduced by the repair.

correctness.justification: assert_file_equals performs exact byte-for-byte comparison against the pre-sorted golden heredoc, enforcing the deterministic-ordering contract. All 12 test paths (changed/added/removed keys, empty values, space-containing values, comment/blank-line filtering, identical snapshots, missing file, usage error, unknown command, malformed input, duplicate key) are exercised with correct exit-status, stdout, and stderr assertions. The ERE character class [A-Za-z0-9._/-] places - at the end, making it literal; \> in the changed-line pattern is evaluated as literal > in bash ERE. No correctness issues found.

coverage.justification: All acceptance checks listed in the sysdiff fixture slice contract are present and unmodified by the repair. The specific regex patterns in assert_diff_prefixes close the prior F-002 secondary-check gap. One pre-existing Low cosmetic gap remains: error-path stderr assertions use assert_contains rather than exact match, but this was present before the repair and is consistent across all error paths.

determinism.justification: All fixture data is defined via inline heredocs with no external files or environment-dependent inputs. LC_ALL=C is exported, ensuring locale-independent behaviour. WORKDIR is a mktemp directory cleaned on EXIT/HUP/INT/TERM. No timestamps, PIDs, or random values appear in any assertion. The test is fully deterministic.

allowed_paths.justification: Both files are untracked additions (git status: ??) inside tests/. No tracked files outside tests/ were modified. All four paths referenced in smoke_manifest.json steps (tests/smoke_start.py, scripts/smoke.sh, tests/test_sysdiff_fixture.sh, tests/check_sysdiff_smoke.py) were confirmed present by path-resolution check.
